microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing automation workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation model across cloud infrastructure.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating engineering, ops, and partner teams end to end.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, restructuring prioritization logic and reducing planning cycle time by 39% while increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly expanding program throughput without added headcount.

Defined measurable success metrics and outcome-driven OKRs for each resilience product area, translating ambiguous recovery scenarios into clear system-behavior requirements that engineering could spec, build, and validate against real-world failure modes.

Enabled leadership to target automation gaps and allocate resources to high-impact areas by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** that tracked operational toil, resolution rates, and workflow efficiency signals across 140+ teams in real time.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable release process that scaled across the robotics platform.

tailoring_notes: Mirrored Flip JD vocabulary throughout: 'system behavior,' 'automation,' 'real-world,' 'outcomes,' 'resolution rates,' 'edge cases,' 'failure modes,' 'end to end,' and 'measurable metrics.'
Reframed Azure resilience work as owning complex automation workflows with AI agents, APIs, and real-world decisioning — directly parallel to Flip's voice AI + automation product surface.
Added a sixth bullet to microsoft_ft to hit max count and fill page density, emphasizing outcome-driven metrics and system-behavior requirements — core to Flip's PM profile.
Bold emphasis targets metrics and outcome nouns (not whole phrases) so recruiter eye lands on impact first, per JD's emphasis on revenue, retention, and resolution.
